title: Shepherd's Pie

overview.description: A traditional British pie made with slow-cooked lamb in a rich gravy, topped with creamy mashed potato and baked until golden. Proper comfort food that's ideal for batch cooking.

ingredients: 2 tsp fresh thyme leaves (or 1 tsp dried)
method: Peel the potatoes, cut into chunks and cook in salted boiling water until tender.
Meanwhile, heat the oil in a large saucepan and brown the lamb mince thoroughly.
Add the onions, carrots and celery and cook for 10 minutes until softened.
Stir in the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the tomato purée and cook for 2 minutes.
Sprinkle over the flour and stir well.
Gradually add the stock, followed by the Worcestershire sauce, thyme and bay leaf.
Simmer gently for 35–45 minutes until the filling has thickened.
Drain the potatoes and mash with the butter and milk until smooth. Season to taste.
Remove the bay leaf from the filling and transfer it to a large ovenproof dish.
Spread the mashed potato evenly over the filling, roughing the surface with a fork.
Bake at 200°C (180°C fan) for 30 minutes until the topping is golden and the filling is bubbling.
Leave to rest for 10 minutes before serving.

learning.did-you-know: A Shepherd's Pie is traditionally made with lamb. When beef is used, the dish is properly called Cottage Pie.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to build a rich meat filling, make smooth mashed potato and create a crisp, golden topping.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A classic family favourite that freezes beautifully and fits perfectly into Saturday batch cooking.
chapman-tips: Allow the filling to reduce properly before topping with the mash.
Drag a fork across the potato to create ridges that brown beautifully in the oven.
A little grated Parmesan on top isn't traditional, but makes an excellent golden crust if you have some to use.
notes.batch-cooking: Bake two pies while the oven is on and freeze one whole or in individual portions for up to three months.
notes.leftovers: Reheat thoroughly in the oven or microwave until piping hot.
